tools.name: homescan_discover
tools.description: Discover all devices on the local network using ARP table. Returns IP, MAC address, and manufacturer for each device found.

refresh.description: If true, performs a ping sweep first to populate ARP cache with all active devices. Takes longer but finds more devices.

tools.name: homescan_device
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific device by IP address

ip.description: IP address of the device to look up

tools.name: homescan_mac_lookup
tools.description: Look up the manufacturer of a device from its MAC address

mac.description: MAC address to look up (format: xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx)

tools.name: homescan_report
tools.description: Generate a comprehensive network discovery report in markdown format, including device summary, categories, and security flags

tools.name: homescan_export
tools.description: Export discovered devices to Obsidian inventory format. Returns an array of files that can be saved to your vault.

tools.name: homescan_diff
tools.description: Compare current network scan to the last scan and show new/removed devices

tools.name: homescan_flagged
tools.description: List devices flagged for security review (unknown manufacturers, Chinese IoT devices, randomized MACs)

findings.title: MCP server exposes tools with completable arguments
findings.description: 4 tool(s) have schema properties that may support completion/complete enumeration.
findings.remediation: Review which argument values are exposed via completion/complete.

findings.title: Server accepts path traversal cursor without validation
findings.description: tools/list accepted cursor='../../../../etc/passwd' and returned a result instead of rejecting it. This may indicate missing cursor validation.
findings.remediation: Validate cursor values; reject non-opaque or path-like cursor strings.

findings.title: Server correctly ignores cancellation of unknown request
findings.description: The server remained responsive after receiving notifications/cancelled for a non-existent request.

findings.title: Cancellation race condition timing detected — server responded normally
findings.description: The request/cancel race did not cause a hang; the server responded to one of the two messages.
findings.remediation: No action required — server handled the race gracefully.
